profile.title: Senior Software Engineer & Technical Lead
profile.summary: Senior software engineer with 12 years of experience designing and scaling distributed systems across fintech, cloud infrastructure, and developer tooling. I've led cross-functional teams of 8–15 engineers, driven platform migrations that reduced infrastructure costs by 40%, and shipped products used by millions. I care deeply about engineering culture, mentorship, and building systems that are reliable by design rather than by accident.

workExperience.title: Staff Software Engineer / Technical Lead
responsibilities: Lead a team of 10 engineers building Meridian's real-time payment processing platform, handling $4.2B in daily transaction volume with 99.997% uptime.
Redesigned the fraud detection pipeline using Apache Flink and Kafka Streams, cutting fraud loss by 31% and reducing false positive rates from 2.1% to 0.4%.
Drove the platform migration from monolithic Rails to event-driven microservices in Go and Python, reducing P99 latency from 840ms to 120ms.
Established engineering-wide observability standards (OpenTelemetry, Grafana, Tempo) adopted by 6 other product teams.
Partnered with Product and Compliance to ship PCI DSS Level 1 certification on schedule, coordinating across 4 engineering squads.

workExperience.title: Senior Software Engineer
responsibilities: Built and maintained the multi-tenant Kubernetes control plane serving 3,000+ enterprise clusters across AWS, GCP, and Azure.
Developed a cluster autoscaling system that reduced average cloud spend per tenant by 28% without impacting SLA commitments.
Implemented a configuration drift detection service in Rust that eliminated a class of production incidents affecting approximately 12 customers per quarter.
Mentored 4 junior engineers through bi-weekly 1:1s, design reviews, and structured onboarding programs; all four were promoted within 18 months.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
responsibilities: Designed and shipped DataPilot's real-time SQL query compiler targeting PostgreSQL, Redshift, and BigQuery, used by 400+ enterprise analytics teams.
Reduced query planning time by 60% through AST caching and query fingerprinting; became a key acquisition justification for Tableau.
Led the integration of DataPilot's connector SDK into Tableau's product after acquisition, coordinating across two engineering organizations.
Contributed a vectorized aggregation optimization to Apache Arrow (accepted upstream) that improved throughput by 2.4x on columnar datasets.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
responsibilities: Built REST and GraphQL APIs in Python (Django/DRF) serving a healthcare workflow automation platform used across 35 hospital networks.
Implemented HIPAA-compliant audit logging and data lineage tracking, passing external compliance audits with zero findings.
Developed an internal scheduling engine that reduced nurse overtime by an average of 6 hours per week per unit, validated via a 4-hospital pilot.

projects.name: Fenrir — Open-Source Distributed Rate Limiter
projects.description: A production-grade, Redis-backed distributed rate limiter library for Go and Python, supporting sliding window, token bucket, and fixed window algorithms. Designed for multi-region deployments with configurable consistency tradeoffs.
outcomes: 3,200+ GitHub stars and 480+ forks within 18 months of release.
Adopted by 14 organizations including two Fortune 500 companies.
Featured in the CNCF newsletter and discussed in two conference talks.

projects.name: QuantumCache — Adaptive Caching Layer
projects.description: A self-tuning caching middleware for Python web applications that uses reinforcement learning to continuously optimize TTL and eviction policies based on observed access patterns and downstream latency signals.
outcomes: Reduced cache miss rates by 44% in A/B testing vs. static TTL configuration.
Deployed in production at Meridian, serving 800M+ cache operations per day.
Presented at PyCon 2024 to an audience of 350 engineers.

projects.name: Ledger Reconciliation Engine
projects.description: An internal financial reconciliation system at Meridian that automatically detects and resolves discrepancies between internal ledgers and external payment network records (Visa, Mastercard, ACH) using deterministic matching and escalation workflows.
outcomes: Reduced manual reconciliation effort from 40 engineer-hours per week to under 2 hours.
Caught $2.3M in incorrectly processed transactions in the first 6 months.
Reduced time-to-detect for settlement discrepancies from 24 hours to under 90 seconds.

projects.name: OpenTrace — Distributed Tracing SDK
projects.description: An OpenTelemetry-compatible tracing SDK for Python microservices with automatic context propagation, sampling, and integration with Datadog, Honeycomb, and Jaeger backends. Built to reduce instrumentation burden to near-zero.
outcomes: Reduced instrumentation time per new service from ~2 days to under 30 minutes at Cloudstone.
Merged two PRs into the upstream OpenTelemetry Python SDK.

education.notes: Thesis: Adaptive Load Balancing in Heterogeneous Distributed Systems. GPA: 3.91. Recipient of the EECS Graduate Excellence Fellowship.
Minor in Mathematics. Dean's List (6 semesters). President, Women in Computing Society (2011–2012).

publications.title: Adaptive Rate Limiting at Scale: Lessons from Four Years in Production
Taming Payment Reconciliation: A Case Study in Event-Driven Financial Systems

awards.title: Engineering Excellence Award
awards.description: Awarded annually to one engineer company-wide for exceptional technical impact and cross-team leadership. Recognized for the fraud detection pipeline redesign and PCI DSS certification effort.

awards.title: Best Paper — Systems Track
awards.description: Recognized for the paper 'Consistency Without Coordination: Conflict-Free Rate State in Geo-Distributed Systems', co-authored with Cloudstone colleagues.

volunteer.description: Teach weekly web development and computer science fundamentals to high school students in underserved San Francisco communities. Co-developed the intermediate Python curriculum used by 12 instructors across 6 schools.
Mentor engineers from underrepresented groups preparing to give their first conference talks or write technical blog posts. Worked with 18 mentees since 2021.
